data.epi_content:
"Is this enough...?"

"Yes, this is enough."

At my nonchalant response, Evan stares at me as if I'm not myself.

Bewildered, with a face that can't comprehend why.

"You... you're not this kind of person."

And those words that followed were ones I didn't want to hear.

That's right, after stripping away, removing, and now even discarding parts of myself, I probably don't look like this lady anymore.

But I couldn't help but change, could I?

No one can blow their own head off while sober.

Anyone can shoot someone else's head if they have a bit of training and anger, if they stop seeing others as human and hunt them instead.

But if you can do that to yourself, you're clearly broken.

What do you think of me?

What kind of person am I?

Tell me.

"Then what kind of person am I?"

When there's no answer, I press for one.

"What kind of person did you think I was?"

Evan's hands start trembling.

"What, a pathetic dog whimpering under some count's daughter without your help?"

"Do you think I came all this way because I don't want to help you, because I don't know the stories going around?!"

Evan gets angry first, perhaps thinking I'm avoiding his question and trying to scold him instead.

Well, I do know what kind of person I used to be.

Anyway, he shouts with a reddened face.

Though there weren't many people around to draw attention, it's still not appropriate to shout in such a public place.

Evan approaches the locker and tries to grab the cord, but when it doesn't come loose, he ignites fire from his fingertips and burns it.

The problem was that the cord was made of flammable material, and the flames traveled up to my neck.

"It hurts, Evan."

When I say this, Evan uses healing magic on me.

Not focusing his magic on one spot, but spreading it throughout my body.

I could feel even the wounds on my arms healing.

Since he didn't concentrate on one area but spread it widely, they didn't fully heal, but still.

"...I was ready to somehow bury that woman who's tormenting you if you'd just say the word.

It might take some time, but I would.

So why aren't you saying anything?

What was the meaning of begging for help back then?

Isn't this what you wanted?"

No, it wasn't.

Those weren't even my words.

Just begging to be saved, that was all.

What can I do? The lady who said those words is slowly falling away day by day.

Probably dissolved in blood, traveling through the pipes all the way to the sewers.

I always wash it away neatly with water in the bathroom.

"Evan, that's not really important.

If you can say I'm not this kind of person, doesn't that mean you know me very well?"

"Yes, you've always said it yourself.

That we must live with common sense, manners, decency, dignity, and courtesy.

That if one can't even do that, they're not human.

So by your own standards..."

He can't bring himself to ask if I'm not human.
data.epi_content2:
No, who would think I'm a normal human?

Someone who gives up their free will and chooses to live in submission to others out of fear, not of death, but of the pain that comes before it—such a person isn't human.

But for me, this is all that living amounts to.

"What common sense?

Even not dying defies common sense, so what are you expecting?

Of course, you wouldn't know. It all becomes like it never happened for you."

Ah, is this incomprehensible?

Whatever. Evan clearly has no intention of leaving me alone.

It would be much better if he just got tired and left.

"What are you talking about?"

"Just nonsense, nonsense that will someday fly away and disappear.

Meaningless drivel. Weak echoes that anyone can silence."

Evan makes a face that shows he doesn't understand.

The headband keeps catching on my ears, which is starting to bother me.

I take off the headband with cute puppy ears and toss it somewhere, wherever my hand reaches.

I hate this feeling of emotions rising.

When I feel emotions intensely, I start exploring my existence and my essence, and what always comes back to me is nothing but pain.

Feeling constricted in my hands, I take off my gloves too.

"It hurts more when you pity me, help me, worry about me.

Even if Lydia burns my mouth with matches, feeds me garbage, or locks me in a dark locker, it hurts more when you look at me with those pitying eyes."

"Then, the ring. Why? Why did you make it?

Why, on the day I came to you, the day I came to get angry, were you the one looking more miserable?"

Right, I slapped Vivian.

And I returned a bit earlier than usual.

How could you understand how I felt when my head wasn't even cut properly, when I had to face the blade once more?

They probably don't cut the arms of Blacks in one go either.

Father was an artist in that regard.

He would casually say that for simple tasks like picking fruit from trees, one arm is enough.

The ring, am I wearing it?

I took it off long ago.

Moved by the fact that you made it for me.

"Did I look miserable? Pitiful? Were you worried?

So that's why you started to bring up my family but stopped.

How gentlemanly of you."

Evan was trembling, probably heated because he came to help but was only getting cursed at.

He grabbed his glasses, took a deep breath to collect his thoughts, and then spoke.

"Why don't you just admit that you're too scared to get angry at the person bullying you, but you're taking it out on the person who came to help because they're easy targets?"

Well.

Too scared to get angry?

That can't be right.

"Why, Lydia is much easier to deal with than you.

With just a flick of my finger, I could put a hole in her forehead."

But you, if I pull the trigger, wouldn't you deflect it with that precious magic of yours?

That's the kind of people you are.

The kind who surpass human understanding and take it for granted.

"What nonsense have you been spouting all this time?! Do you just want to keep wearing this dog collar?"

Evan angrily waves the already burned dog collar around.

His body swayed with each movement.

"I don't want to talk nonsense, but I've experienced something shocking twice.
data.epi_content3:
Why am I like this?

Because I'm depressed, because I feel like I'm going crazy, I still do, and because the only lifeline I can see is you, even though I'm cutting it myself."

It's contradictory.

Wanting help but not pity, wanting someone to care but not worry.

A person becomes twisted.

Perhaps Erica needed to become something inhuman in order to survive.

"You're not normal, no matter how..."

I cut him off, not wanting to hear the rest.

"Of course not, but aren't you a little happy?"

"Happy?"

"Oh, one obstacle that was getting in the way of you and Vivian just exploded on its own."

I speak mockingly in a voice that sounds like it belongs in a play.

Like a court jester facing a noble lord weaker than himself, I clap my hands with exaggerated laughter.

I didn't know I could make such expressions.

"I'll help you out of the friendship we've had, but if you had any shame, you wouldn't even think of approaching me again, right?

Since you always talked about manners, common sense, and decency, hahaha!"

"You know that's not what I meant..."

As Evan approached calmly and rationally, I lashed out emotionally.

"Why, did you want to somehow fill that pathetic pride of yours?

I hear there are quite a few people hanging around Vivian these days, so your self-esteem has dropped, and now look at you wagging your tail."

Evan's response wasn't like before, where we would raise our voices and hurl insults at each other.

He just glanced at me with a troubled look, stared into space for a while with slightly reddened eyes, and then just ran his large hand down his face.

"...Fine."

He looks at me with bitter eyes.

"Don't look at me like that."

"If you feel guilty when I look at you, Erica, that's your problem.

Like commoners who don't mind when police officers look at them normally, but instinctively shrink when they're looked at after committing a crime."

"There's nothing wrong with me.

It's everything around me that's the problem.

At first, I thought I was the problem, but it was just everything around me."

"Do you really have no problems at all?

You kick away those who try to help, push away those who try to get close—you don't see any problem with that?"

"Then, what do you want me to do?

It was you who pushed me away when I tried to get close.

Only Vivian came looking for me, how can you say such things...?"

I tried not to cry.

I feel like I've become much more tearful lately.

So I tried to live like this, crawling on the ground, giving up my humanity, but now you say I'm not this kind of person.

This Erica isn't Erica.

Then what am I supposed to do?

You're basically telling me to die.

Because someone like me isn't even Erica.

"Erica...? Evan...?"

Suddenly, a familiar girl's voice is heard, and when I look over, Vivian is peeking into the classroom with just her face showing.

And in that moment, I heard the sound of a tightly stretched thread in my mind snapping.

"Ah, I can't take this anymore."
